body.question_1: How would you rate the overall quality of teaching in your courses this semester so far?
body.answer_1: I have observed several insufficient aspects of teaching quality in my Journalism courses at the School of Communication this semester that I feel compelled to bring to attention. Certain lectures are delivered without adequate structure, making it difficult to follow the logical progression of content, and some lecturers rely heavily on reading directly from slides rather than elaborating or encouraging discussion. Assessment criteria are sometimes communicated after assignments are already underway, which creates unnecessary confusion and unfair evaluation conditions. I strongly urge the institution to invest in faculty development programs focused on modern pedagogy, student engagement techniques, and constructive feedback practices to address these shortcomings urgently.

body.question_2: How well does the course content align with current industry standards and your career aspirations?
body.answer_2: The curriculum for my Journalism program at the School of Communication is well-designed and clearly aligned with current industry demands and emerging professional trends in the field. Course structures are logical, building progressively on foundational concepts before introducing advanced topics, which gives students a solid intellectual scaffold. The balance between theoretical foundations and practical application is impressive, with project-based assessments, case studies, and laboratory work that directly reflect real-world professional scenarios I will encounter in my career. I particularly value the inclusion of contemporary tools and technologies, which ensures graduates are competitive and well-prepared for the demands of the modern job market.

body.question_3: How effective are the assessment methods (assignments, quizzes, exams) in evaluating your understanding of the subject matter?
body.answer_3: Assessment practices in my Journalism program at the School of Communication are generally reasonable, though there is meaningful room for improvement in both consistency and transparency. Some modules employ well-designed assessments that genuinely test comprehension and application, while others rely too heavily on high-stakes end-of-semester examinations that do not adequately reflect students' ongoing learning throughout the term. Timely feedback is provided by some lecturers but is inconsistent across the department. Standardizing minimum feedback turnaround times and encouraging a more diverse portfolio of assessment types would significantly improve the learning experience for all students.

body.question_4: How would you describe the availability and responsiveness of your lecturers outside of scheduled class time?
body.answer_4: Lecturer availability and responsiveness at the School of Communication has been one of the strongest aspects of my Journalism student experience this semester, and I genuinely commend it. Faculty members consistently maintain clearly communicated office hours, respond to email inquiries within one to two business days, and frequently offer additional consultation sessions during busy assessment periods. The approachable attitude of lecturers creates a supportive learning environment where students feel comfortable raising questions, discussing challenges, and seeking guidance on both academic work and career-related concerns. This level of accessibility significantly enhances engagement and outcomes and is something I hope will be maintained and encouraged across all departments.

body.question_5: How satisfied are you with the learning resources and materials provided for your courses this semester?
body.answer_5: Learning resources for my Journalism program at the School of Communication are satisfactory, with some courses providing excellent, well-curated materials and others offering minimal guidance beyond the core required textbook. The library's digital collection is reasonable in scope but has some notable gaps in specialized resources relevant to advanced topics in our field. The LMS functions adequately as a distribution platform, though its use varies significantly between lecturers, creating an inconsistent experience across modules. A more standardized approach to resource provision, with minimum content requirements per course, would considerably improve the consistency and quality of the learning environment.

body.question_6: How would you rate the IT infrastructure and digital learning tools provided by the university to support your studies?
body.answer_6: The IT infrastructure at the School of Communication, maintained by the IT Support Department, performs at a moderate level that meets basic student needs but falls short of what is required to fully support a modern, technology-dependent academic environment. Wi-Fi connectivity is generally stable in primary teaching spaces but becomes inconsistent during peak usage hours and in peripheral campus locations. Software availability in computer labs is adequate for core coursework but limited for students requiring specialized applications for advanced projects. Expanding bandwidth capacity, updating hardware on a regular replacement cycle, and improving helpdesk responsiveness would meaningfully elevate the digital learning experience for the entire student community.

body.question_7: How efficient have you found the academic administrative processes such as registration, grade appeals, and scheduling?
body.answer_7: The academic administrative processes managed by the Academic Affairs Office at the School of Communication have been remarkable this semester, and I acknowledge the efficiency and transparency with which they are handled. Course registration was smooth and intuitive via the student portal, with clear instructions provided well in advance of enrollment deadlines. Grade appeals and transcript requests are processed promptly and communicated through official channels with regular status updates that reduce student anxiety. Administrative staff are approachable, knowledgeable, and genuinely helpful when students encounter difficulties navigating university policies or procedures. The overall administrative experience reflects a student-centered approach that I believe enhances the broader university experience significantly.

body.question_8: How would you evaluate the quality and accessibility of student support services currently available at this university?
body.answer_8: Student support services at the School of Communication are fair in their scope and delivery, covering the essential categories but lacking depth and proactivity in several important areas that affect student well-being. Counseling services exist and are functional, but appointment availability is limited and wait times are often longer than appropriate for students in need. Financial support processes are reasonably accessible but require significant paperwork and involve delays that reduce their effectiveness in urgent situations. Investing in additional counseling staff, simplifying support request processes, and proactively promoting available services — especially during high-stress periods like mid-terms — would markedly improve the student experience.

body.question_9: How well does the university's physical environment and facilities support your daily learning experience?
body.answer_9: The physical facilities at the School of Communication, under the responsibility of the Facilities Management, present inconsistent conditions that negatively impact the daily learning experience of students. Several lecture halls have malfunctioning air conditioning systems that make studying in warm months genuinely uncomfortable and distracting. Toilet facilities in older buildings are poorly maintained and frequently reported without timely resolution from the facilities team. Study spaces are chronically insufficient relative to the student population, particularly during examination periods when demand peaks significantly. I urge the university to prioritize facilities maintenance budgets, establish a transparent issue-reporting system, and expand study space capacity as part of its next capital improvement plan.

body.question_10: What specific improvements would you most like to see implemented for the remainder of this semester?
body.answer_10: I have very few concerns to raise about my overall experience in the Journalism program at the School of Communication, and I genuinely believe this institution provides an excellent educational environment that prepares students well for their future careers. If I were to recommend any improvement, I would suggest further expanding industry partnership opportunities — additional mentorship programs, sponsored capstone projects, and guest lecture series from senior professionals would enrich an already strong academic offering. Increasing the frequency of student feedback collection and demonstrating visible action on that feedback would further strengthen trust between students and the institution. Overall, I am proud to be a student here and would highly recommend this university to future applicants without hesitation.
